name: Van Cortlandt Cooperative
description_short: Van Cortlandt Cooperative Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and online banking with NCUA insurance protection.
description_long:
Van Cortlandt Cooperative Federal Credit Union (VCCFCU) is a federally chartered credit union celebrating its 90th year of operation as of 2026. The institution operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. VCCFCU serves its members through a physical branch with limited teller hours and comprehensive digital banking services.

The credit union offers a full suite of consumer financial products including share deposit accounts (savings and checking), youth accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts, share certificates, and burial accounts. On the lending side, VCCFCU provides personal loans, debt consolidation loans, open lines of credit, seasonal loans, share-secured loans, vehicle loans, mortgages, and home equity loans. Members also have access to a VISA Classic credit card and VISA debit cards. Current deposit rates range from 0.30% to 3.03% APY depending on account type, while loan rates span from 3.50% APR for share-secured loans to 7.99% APR for debt consolidation products.

VCCFCU distinguishes itself through its non-profit, member-owned structure which positions it as providing lower-cost financial services compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes security through SSL encryption, 128-bit encryption standards, and encrypted security codes. Members benefit from NCUA federal insurance protection up to $250,000 on deposits. The credit union offers online banking, mobile banking, bill payment services, eStatements, and Zelle integration. They provide member resources including savings calculators, loan calculators, mortgage calculators, and exclusive member discounts.

A key limitation is the restricted branch accessibility—teller windows operate only Monday-Thursday 2pm-8pm and Tuesday-Wednesday 9am-12pm, which may be inconvenient for many consumers. The website does not clearly disclose membership eligibility criteria or membership fees. Some loan products lack published rates on the main website, requiring members to apply or contact the institution directly. As a smaller credit union, VCCFCU may have limited product sophistication compared to national financial institutions.
services: Online banking, mobile banking, bill payment, eStatements
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure typically results in lower fees and competitive rates compared to for-profit banks
Deposits federally insured up to $250,000 by NCUA with full U.S. Government backing
Competitive youth savings rate of 3.03% APY for younger account holders
Comprehensive loan products including debt consolidation, mortgages, and home equity lines of credit
Strong security protocols including SSL encryption, 128-bit encryption, and encrypted security codes
Multiple digital banking channels: online banking, mobile banking, bill payment, eStatements, and Zelle integration
Offers Visa debit cards and Visa Classic credit card for member convenience
cons: Very limited teller window hours (only 2pm-8pm Mon/Thu and 9am-12pm Tue/Wed) restrict in-person access
Membership eligibility criteria not clearly disclosed on website, creating uncertainty about who can join
Many loan products lack published APR rates on website—members must apply or contact directly for pricing
As a smaller credit union, likely offers less product variety and digital sophistication than national institutions
No mention of monthly account maintenance fees or other charges despite fee schedule reference
best_for: Individuals seeking a member-owned financial institution with non-profit governance and lower costs
Borrowers needing debt consolidation or mortgage services with competitive rates
Parents opening youth savings accounts, which earn 3.03% APY—significantly higher than typical youth accounts
Members prioritizing online and mobile banking capabilities with strong security encryption standards
diagnosis: VCCFCU is best for individuals seeking a member-owned, not-for-profit alternative to traditional banks with competitive deposit and loan rates backed by federal NCUA insurance. The primary caveat is severely limited branch hours (maximum 8 hours Mon/Thu, 3 hours Tue/Wed) that make in-person banking difficult; this institution is viable only for members comfortable conducting most transactions online or via phone.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Van Cortlandt Cooperative — NCUA-insured credit union in BRONX, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
